tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57918,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: README Key Concepts missing convenience generators for ResponseEventStream","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #57895 (`ecd2108`) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #57895 added convenience generators to `ResponseEventStream` — `OutputItemMessage()` (string and `IAsyncEnumerable` overloads), `OutputItemFunctionCall()`, and `OutputItemReasoningItem()` — and restructured Samples 3, 4, and 6 with a convenience-first pattern. The changes promote convenience generators as the recommended approach for most `ResponseEventStream` use cases, relegating the full builder API to advanced scenarios only. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` **Key Concepts > ResponseEventStream** section shows the full builder pattern (`stream.AddOutputItemMessage()`, `message.EmitAdded()`, etc.) as the primary example, with no mention of the new convenience generators (`stream.OutputItemMessage()`) - The README description "Use `ResponseEventStream` when you need multiple output types … fine-grained delta control … custom Response properties" no longer reflects the convenience-first hierarchy introduced in this PR - The README omits the streaming tokens overload `stream.OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable)`, which is now a first-class supported pattern documented in Sample 3 - The three-tier mental model (TextResponse → convenience generators → full builder) is fully documented in `samples/Sample3_FullControlResponseStream.md` but absent from the package README that most users read first 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File to edit:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` In the **Key concepts > ResponseEventStream** section, replace the current block that reads: > **`ResponseEventStream` — full control over every SSE event:** > > Use `ResponseEventStream` when you need multiple output types (reasoning, function calls), fine-grained delta control, or custom Response properties. > > ` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_FullControl ` with content that introduces the three-tier hierarchy: 1. `TextResponse` — simple text-only responses (already documented, no change) 2. `ResponseEventStream` with **convenience generators** — recommended for most non-text-only scenarios (NEW: `OutputItemMessage`, `OutputItemFunctionCall`, `OutputItemReasoningItem`) 3. `ResponseEventStream` with **full builder control** — for advanced scenarios (custom item properties, interleaved non-event work, multiple summary parts) The convenience generator example to add is already captured in snippet `Responses_Sample3_GreetingHandlerConvenience`: ```csharp public class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler { public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync( CreateResponse request, ResponseContext context, [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken) { await Task.CompletedTask; var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request); stream.Response.Temperature = 0.7; yield return stream.EmitCreated(); yield return stream.EmitInProgress(); foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($"Hello! You said: \"{request.GetInputText()}\"")) yield return evt; yield return stream.EmitCompleted(); } } ``` Also add or reference the streaming-tokens overload from snippet `Responses_Sample3_StreamingGreetingHandler`: ```csharp // Stream tokens as they arrive — each chunk becomes a delta event. await foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage( GenerateTokensAsync(request.GetInputText(), cancellationToken), cancellationToken)) { yield return evt; } ``` Keep the existing full-control builder snippet (`Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_FullControl`) below, clearly labeled as "With full event control" for advanced scenarios. Update the prose description to match Sample 3's explanation: > Use convenience generators when you need to emit function calls, reasoning items, or multiple outputs but don't need per-event control. Drop down to the builder API only when you need to set custom properties on output items before `EmitAdded()`, interleave non-event work between individual delta/done calls, or emit multiple summary parts within a single reasoning item. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet for the convenience generator approach should be written in a test file so the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can inject it into the README automatically. **Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleReadme.cs` (create or edit — check if a ReadMe sample test file already exists; if not, create one following the pattern in `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`) Add a test method wrapping the convenience generator example: `````csharp [Test] public async Task ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience() { #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience // Use OutputItemMessage() for non-text-only responses without per-event control. // The convenience generator handles all inner events automatically. public class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler { public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync( CreateResponse request, ResponseContext context, [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken) { await Task.CompletedTask; var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request); yield return stream.EmitCreated(); yield return stream.EmitInProgress(); var input = request.GetInputText(); foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($"Hello! You said: \"{input}\"")) yield return evt; yield return stream.EmitCompleted(); } } #endregion await Task.CompletedTask; } ``` **README placeholder** to insert in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`, under the `ResponseEventStream` key concept, after the prose introduction of convenience generators: ````` ````` ``` ````` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently has all required sections. Only the **Key Concepts > ResponseEventStream** subsection needs to be updated: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started — Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started — Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started — Configure the server | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — ResponseHandler | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — TextResponse | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — ResponseEventStream (convenience generators) | ❌ **Missing** | | Key concepts — ResponseEventStream (full builder) | ✅ Present (but needs updated guidance text) | | Key concepts — Streaming & Background Modes | ✅ Present | | Key concepts — Response Lifecycle | ✅ Present | | Examples | ✅ Present | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm README was updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no new public API was added in this doc change, but run to confirm no regressions 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24116375459/agentic_workflow) · ● 367.8K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57918","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T03:46:11Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:46:11Z"}

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The README for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is missing documentation for the new convenience generators (`OutputItemMessage`, `OutputItemFunctionCall`, `OutputItemReasoningItem`) added to `ResponseEventStream` in PR #57895, leaving the primary user-facing docs out of sync with the recommended convenience-first usage pattern.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`
**Affected API:** `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators (`OutputItemMessage`, `OutputItemFunctionCall`, `OutputItemReasoningItem`)
**Scenarios:**
- README Key Concepts > ResponseEventStream still shows full builder pattern as the primary example, with no mention of the new convenience generators
- Streaming tokens overload `stream.OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable<string>)` is completely absent from the README
- Three-tier mental model (TextResponse → convenience generators → full builder) is documented in sample files but not in the README

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` to introduce convenience generators as the recommended approach and demote the full builder API to an advanced-scenario section.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue, not a code bug. The implementation guide in the issue body is detailed and actionable:

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Update the **Key Concepts > ResponseEventStream** section in `README.md` to introduce the three-tier hierarchy (TextResponse → convenience generators → full builder)
2. Add or verify a snippet `Responses_ReadMe_EchoHandler_Convenience` in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/SampleReadme.cs` and insert the README placeholder
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to inject the snippet into the README, then `dotnet build` and `dotnet test` to verify no regressions
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — the issue concerns SDK package documentation and code samples, not a management-plane or provisioning package
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — the package `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` lives under `sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` service label per CODEOWNERS
- **Confidence:** High — the package name and service directory are explicitly stated in the issue; no ambiguity between services or categories
</details>
